note: Golden set for the compaction benchmark. must_have = signatures that MUST survive compaction (recall gate). qa = questions answerable from the docs (LLM-judge). lookup = the grep-tier lines docs-distill would emit.

qa.q: How do you run startup and shutdown code in a modern FastAPI app?
qa.reference: Use a lifespan async context manager (an @asynccontextmanager function that yields) and pass it as FastAPI(lifespan=...). The older @app.on_event("startup")/@app.on_event("shutdown") handlers are deprecated.

qa.q: How do you provide a database session to routes so cleanup runs after the response?
qa.reference: Write a dependency function that creates the session, yields it inside try/finally (closing it in finally), and inject it into routes with Depends(get_db).

qa.q: How do you return a 400 error from inside a route handler?
How do you group related routes under a common path prefix and attach them to the app?
qa.reference: Create an APIRouter(prefix="/...") , define routes on it, then register it with app.include_router(router) at module level.

lookup: fastapi | FastAPI(title, lifespan) | create app instance
fastapi | APIRouter(prefix, tags) | group routes; register via app.include_router()
fastapi | HTTPException(status_code, detail) | raise an HTTP error
fastapi | Query(default, ...) | declare a query parameter
fastapi | Body(...) | required JSON body (Body(default) = optional)
fastapi | BackgroundTasks.add_task(fn, *args) | run work after the response
